_meta.description: Fixed liturgical texts for Bridegroom Matins — served Mon/Tue/Wed evenings of Holy Week
_meta.note: The Bridegroom services are Matins anticipated on the preceding evening. Monday evening = Matins of Tuesday, etc. The Bridegroom Troparion and Exaposteilarion are the same all three nights; the kontakion, ikos, and stichera vary.

opening.exclamation: Blessed is our God always, now and ever and unto ages of ages.

sixPsalms._note: Psalms 3, 37, 62, 87, 102, 142. Read from psalter.json at assembly time.
sixPsalms.intro:
Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will among men. (×3)

O Lord, Thou shalt open my lips, and my mouth shall declare Thy praise. (×2)
sixPsalms.midGlory:
Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever and unto ages of ages. Amen.

Alleluia, alleluia, alleluia! Glory to Thee, O God! (×3)

Lord, have mercy. (×3)

Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever and unto ages of ages. Amen.

alleluia._note: During Holy Week, Alleluia replaces 'God is the Lord' at Matins.
verses: Out of the night my spirit seeks Thee early, O God, for Thy commandments are a light upon the earth.
Learn righteousness, ye that dwell upon the earth.
Jealousy shall seize upon an uninstructed people.

troparion.label: Troparion of the Bridegroom
troparion.text:
Behold the Bridegroom cometh at midnight,
and blessed is the servant whom He shall find watching;
and again, unworthy is the servant whom He shall find heedless.
Beware, therefore, O my soul, do not be weighed down with sleep,
lest you be given up to death and lest you be shut out of the Kingdom.
But rouse yourself, crying:
Holy, holy, holy art Thou, O our God!
Through the Theotokos, have mercy on us!

kathismata._note: Sessional hymns sung after the kathisma readings. These vary by night.

monday.text:
Behold, the Bridegroom comes in the middle of the night,
and blessed is the servant whom He shall find awake.
But unworthy is he whom He shall find careless.
Beware then, O my soul, lest thou fall into deep slumber,
and the door of the Kingdom be shut against thee,
and thou be delivered to death.
But be wakeful, crying:
Holy, holy, holy art Thou, O God:
through the Theotokos, have mercy on us.

tuesday.text:
The harlot came to Thee, O Lover of mankind,
pouring myrrh mixed with tears upon Thy feet.
At Thy command she was delivered from the stench of her evil deeds.
But the ungrateful disciple, though breathing Thy grace,
rejected it and wallowed in filth,
selling Thee for the sake of greed.
Glory to Thy compassion, O Christ!

wednesday.text:
Judas the betrayer, deceitful and crafty,
with a treacherous kiss delivered the Savior, the Lord of all.
He sold as a slave the Master of all creation,
and like a lamb to the slaughter, the Son and God of all was led.
He alone is most compassionate!

kontakion.text:
Consider, O my soul, the end which is to come,
and tremble before the cutting of the fig tree,
and labor diligently with the talent given to thee, O wretched one:
watch and pray,
that we may not remain outside the bridal chamber of Christ.

ikos.text:
Why art thou idle, O my wretched soul?
Why dost thou dream endlessly of things that do not profit?
Why dost thou busy thyself with things that pass away?
The last hour is at hand, and we shall be parted from all things here below.
While thou hast time, come to thy senses and cry:
I have sinned against Thee, O my Savior. Do not cut me down
like the barren fig tree,
but in Thy compassion, O Christ, have mercy on me,
who cry in fear:
Let us not remain outside the bridal chamber of Christ.

stichera._note: Lauds stichera for Monday evening (Matins of Tuesday). Theme: the barren fig tree.

hymns.verse: Let every breath praise the Lord. Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise Him in the highest!
hymns.text:
O Lord, coming to Thy voluntary Passion,
Thou didst say to Thy disciples:
"If you could not watch one hour with Me,
how did you promise to die for Me?
See how Judas does not sleep,
but hastens to betray Me to the lawless.
Arise and pray, lest any of you deny Me
when you see Me on the Cross."
O long-suffering Lord, glory to Thee!

hymns.verse: Praise Him for His mighty acts; praise Him according to the multitude of His greatness!
hymns.text:
Judas, the slave and deceiver,
the disciple and betrayer,
the friend and devil, was revealed by his deeds.
For following the Master, he plotted betrayal within himself.
He said: "I will deliver Him up and gain the money promised."
He desired the myrrh to be sold,
and Jesus to be taken by deceit.
He gave a kiss and delivered Christ.
And the Lamb of God, the Son of the Father,
alone most compassionate,
was led as a sheep to the slaughter.

glory.text:
The Lamb whom Isaiah proclaimed
comes voluntarily to the slaughter.
He gives His back to scourging,
His cheeks to blows.
He does not turn His face from the shame of spitting,
and is condemned to a shameful death.
He Who is sinless accepts all things willingly,
that He may grant to all the Resurrection from the dead.

now.text:
Most blessed art thou, O Virgin Theotokos,
for through Him Who was incarnate of thee,
Hades is led captive,
Adam recalled, the curse destroyed,
Eve freed, death slain,
and we are given life.
Wherefore, we cry out in praise:
Blessed art Thou, O Christ our God, Who art thus well pleased! Glory to Thee!

kontakion.text:
I have transgressed more than the harlot, O Good One,
yet never have I offered Thee my flowing tears.
But in silence I fall down before Thee and with love I kiss Thy most pure feet,
beseeching Thee as Master to grant me remission of sins;
and I cry to Thee, O Savior:
Deliver me from the filth of my evil deeds.

ikos.text:
The woman who was formerly prodigal
now appeared chaste, hating the deeds of shameful sin
and the pleasures of the body.
She pondered the great shame and the judgment of torment
that harlots and profligates shall endure.
Of their number I am first, and I am afraid.
But foolishly I continue in my evil habit.
The sinful woman, in fear, hastened to her Deliverer and cried to Him:
O merciful Lover of mankind, deliver me from the filth of my evil deeds.

stichera._note: Lauds stichera for Tuesday evening (Matins of Wednesday). Theme: the sinful woman and Judas's betrayal.

hymns.verse: Let every breath praise the Lord. Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise Him in the highest!
hymns.text:
The sinful woman ran to buy precious myrrh
to anoint the Benefactor.
She cried to the perfume dealer:
"Give me myrrh, that I may anoint
Him Who has wiped away all my sins."

hymns.verse: Praise Him for His mighty acts; praise Him according to the multitude of His greatness!
hymns.text:
The sinful woman brought myrrh and tears together
and poured them at the feet of the Master.
She kissed them and was made clean at once.
God granted her remission of her sins,
for He endured the Cross for the sake of us all.

glory.text:
The sinful woman approached Thee, the Lover of mankind.
Bathing Thy feet with tears, she cried:
"Have mercy on me, O Master!
I beseech Thee, destroy the great burden of my sins!
As Thou didst accept the tears of Peter,
accept mine as well, O Savior,
for Thou art the One Who callest all men to salvation."

now.text:
Most blessed art thou, O Virgin Theotokos,
for through Him Who was incarnate of thee,
Hades is led captive,
Adam recalled, the curse destroyed,
Eve freed, death slain,
and we are given life.
Wherefore, we cry out in praise:
Blessed art Thou, O Christ our God, Who art thus well pleased! Glory to Thee!

kontakion.text:
I have transgressed more than the harlot, O Good One,
yet never have I offered Thee my flowing tears.
But in silence I fall down before Thee and with love I kiss Thy most pure feet,
beseeching Thee as Master to grant me remission of sins;
and I cry to Thee, O Savior:
Deliver me from the filth of my evil deeds.

ikos.text:
The woman who was formerly prodigal
now appeared chaste, hating the deeds of shameful sin
and the pleasures of the body.
She pondered the great shame and the judgment of torment
that harlots and profligates shall endure.
Of their number I am first, and I am afraid.
But foolishly I continue in my evil habit.
The sinful woman, in fear, hastened to her Deliverer and cried to Him:
O merciful Lover of mankind, deliver me from the filth of my evil deeds.

stichera._note: Lauds stichera for Wednesday evening (Matins of Thursday). Theme: the anointing at Bethany and Judas's bargain.

hymns.verse: Let every breath praise the Lord. Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise Him in the highest!
hymns.text:
While the sinful woman was bringing the myrrh,
the disciple was making an agreement with the lawless.
She rejoiced to pour out what was very precious;
he hastened to sell the One Who is above all price.
She acknowledged Christ as Lord;
he severed himself from the Master.
She was set free, but Judas became a slave of the enemy.
Grievous was his lack of love!
Great was her repentance!
Grant me such repentance, O Savior,
Who didst suffer for our sake,// and save us!

hymns.verse: Praise Him for His mighty acts; praise Him according to the multitude of His greatness!
hymns.text:
What wickedness, Judas!
The harlot brought myrrh to anoint the Master;
and thou didst come to an agreement with the lawless.
She poured out her precious possession,
and thou didst sell the Precious One.
She recognized the Master;
thou didst separate thyself from Him.
She was set free; thou didst become the slave of the devil.
Fearful is thy carelessness, O Judas!
Grant such repentance to us, O God,// and save us!

glory.label: Hymn of Kassiane
glory._note: The famous Hymn of Kassiane — the most beloved sticheron of Holy Week. Sung on Wednesday evening.
glory.text:
O Lord, the woman who had fallen into many sins,
perceiving Thy divinity,
took upon herself the duty of a myrrh-bearer.
With lamentation she brought sweet-smelling myrrh to Thee before Thy burial.
"Woe is me," she said,
"for night surrounds me, dark and moonless,
and stings my lustful passion with the love of sin.
Accept the fountain of my tears,
O Thou Who drawest the water of the sea through the clouds.
Bow down Thine ear to the sighing of my heart,
O Thou Who didst bow the heavens by Thine ineffable condescension.
I will kiss Thy most pure feet
and wipe them with the tresses of my head,
those feet at whose sound Eve hid herself in fear
when she heard Thee walking in Paradise in the cool of the day.
Who can search out the multitude of my sins,
or the depths of Thy judgments, O my Savior?
Do not despise me, Thy servant,
for Thy mercy is boundless."

now.text:
Most blessed art thou, O Virgin Theotokos,
for through Him Who was incarnate of thee,
Hades is led captive,
Adam recalled, the curse destroyed,
Eve freed, death slain,
and we are given life.
Wherefore, we cry out in praise:
Blessed art Thou, O Christ our God, Who art thus well pleased! Glory to Thee!

exaposteilarion.label: Exaposteilarion of the Bridegroom
exaposteilarion._note: Sung three times — same all three nights.
exaposteilarion.text:
I see Thy bridal chamber adorned, O my Savior,
and I have no wedding garment that I may enter.
O Giver of Light, enlighten the vesture of my soul,
and save me!

greatDoxology._note: The Great Doxology is read (not sung) at Holy Week Matins.
greatDoxology.text:
Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace, good will among men.

We praise Thee, we bless Thee, we worship Thee, we glorify Thee, we give thanks to Thee for Thy great glory.

O Lord God, heavenly King, God the Father Almighty; O Lord, the only-begotten Son, Jesus Christ; and O Holy Spirit.

O Lord God, Lamb of God, Son of the Father, that takest away the sin of the world, have mercy on us; Thou that takest away the sins of the world, receive our prayer; Thou that sittest at the right hand of the Father, have mercy on us.

For Thou only art holy, Thou only art the Lord, Jesus Christ, to the glory of God the Father. Amen.

Every day will I bless Thee, and I will praise Thy Name forever and ever.

Vouchsafe, O Lord, to keep us this day without sin.

Blessed art Thou, O Lord, the God of our fathers, and praised and glorified is Thy Name forever. Amen.

Let Thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, as we have set our hope on Thee.

Blessed art Thou, O Lord; teach me Thy statutes.
Blessed art Thou, O Master; make me to understand Thy statutes.
Blessed art Thou, O Holy One; enlighten me with Thy statutes.

Thy mercy, O Lord, endureth forever. O despise not the works of Thy hands.

To Thee belongeth worship, to Thee belongeth praise, to Thee belongeth glory: to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, now and ever and unto ages of ages. Amen.

dismissal.monday: May He Who goes to His voluntary Passion for our salvation, Christ our true God, through the prayers of His all-pure Mother, and of all the saints, have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loveth mankind.
dismissal.tuesday: May He Who goes to His voluntary Passion for our salvation, Christ our true God, through the prayers of His all-pure Mother, and of all the saints, have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loveth mankind.
dismissal.wednesday: May He Who for our salvation endured spitting, and scourging, and buffeting, Christ our true God, through the prayers of His all-pure Mother, and of all the saints, have mercy on us and save us, for He is good and loveth mankind.
